2605.00890.title: Skeleton-Based Posture Classification to Promote Safer Walker-Assisted Gait in Older Adults
2605.00890.paper_summary: Falls among older adults are a significant public health concern, leading to severe injuries, loss of independence, and increased healthcare costs. This study evaluates the effectiveness of various models, including a Geometric approach, XGBoost, SVM, and several deep learning architectures, in classifying walker usage, standing vs. sitting, and posture for smart walkers used. Geometric and XGBoost were the top performers. XGBoost achieved near-perfect training accuracy in binary classification tasks, with 99.84% for walker choice and 99.69% for standing vs. sitting. For posture classification, Geometric approach attained 89.9% accuracy for 8 postures, and XGBoost obtained 99.24% during training for 17 postures. Deep learning models such as the 4-layer CNN and Encoder-Decoder CNN also demonstrated strong performance in binary classification, with accuracies above 98%. This study underscores the potential of machine learning to enhance human-robot interaction in smart walkers, particularly for fall prevention.
2605.00890.paper_summary_zh: 跌倒對於老年人來說是一個重大的公共衛生問題，導致嚴重的傷害、失去獨立性以及增加的醫療成本。這項研究評估了各種模型的有效性，包括幾何方法、XGBoost、支持向量機（SVM）以及幾種深度學習架構，用於分類助行器的使用、站立與坐下，以及智能助行器的姿勢。幾何方法和XGBoost是表現最好的模型。XGBoost在二元分類任務中達到了近乎完美的訓練準確率，助行器選擇的準確率為99.84%，站立與坐下的準確率為99.69%。在姿勢分類方面，幾何方法對8種姿勢的準確率達到了89.9%，而XGBoost在訓練中對17種姿勢的準確率為99.24%。深度學習模型，如4層CNN和編碼器-解碼器CNN，在二元分類中也展現了強勁的表現，準確率均超過98%。這項研究強調了機器學習在增強智能助行器中人機互動的潛力，特別是在防止跌倒方面。
2605.00890.author: Sergio D. Sierra M. et.al.
2605.00890.authors: Sergio D. Sierra M., Monica Sinha, Marcela Múnera, Carlos A. Cifuentes
